description: Flowline failure — brine release to bare soil, approximate 420 BBL. Caliche surface affected. Operator self-reported within 24h.
Tank battery overflow during high-volume pump operation. Approx 210 BBL produced water reached unpaved lease road and adjacent soil.
Pipeline rupture at weld joint. 850 BBL produced brine released across ~0.4 acres of rangeland. Soil remediation initiated.
Stuffing box failure on production wellhead. 115 BBL brine released, contained within lease.
High-pressure disposal line failure. 1,200 BBL produced water impacted approximately 0.8 acres of rangeland adjacent to lease road. Remediation crew deployed within 48h.
Produced water impoundment berms overtopped during heavy rainfall event. 340 BBL released off-pad.
Gathering system pipe rupture. Large-volume produced water release. 2,800 BBL estimated. Confirmed by aerial survey. Soil and vegetation mortality documented within 1.1 acres.
Production separator overflow. 760 BBL brine released, partially recovered. Impacted soil samples showed elevated chloride levels.
Flex hose connection failure on produced water transfer truck. 490 BBL released on location.
Injection well annulus failure — surface casing vent flow of produced water. Estimated 1,540 BBL released before mechanical intervention.
Weld failure on surface pipeline at tank battery. 220 BBL produced brine released to caliche surface. Full remediation completed.
Flowback water tank overflow during completion operations. 680 BBL produced water impacted nearby soil. Operator reported within 2 hours.
Major buried pipeline rupture on produced water gathering system. Estimated 3,200 BBL released across 1.3 acres. Soil core sampling confirmed deep infiltration.
Meter run connection failure. 310 BBL produced water to soil, within containment berm. No offsite migration confirmed.
Produced water hauler truck rollover incident. 175 BBL released to roadside and adjacent rangeland.
Tank battery valve left open unintentionally during site maintenance. 540 BBL produced water released. Soil remediation ongoing.
Line rupture on high-pressure produced water gathering. 1,880 BBL discharged. Satellite anomaly and ground-truth confirmed brine signature over 0.6 acres.
Stuffing box leak at wellhead. 290 BBL produced brine accumulated in wellhead pit before discovery.
Above-ground production tank structural failure. 410 BBL produced water release, impacting approximately 0.3 acres.
Erosion of secondary containment allowed produced water to escape lease boundary during storm surge. 660 BBL estimated off-lease.
Buried pipeline pinhole leak from corrosion. Slow release estimated at 720 BBL over multi-day period before detection. Surface staining confirmed.
Valve failure on production separator. 1,050 BBL produced water released to soil. Chloride soil analysis confirmed brine infiltration at 0.9m depth.
Injection tubing leak allowed produced water to accumulate at surface. 185 BBL brine released to caliche.
Flowline failure between separator and tank — 430 BBL produced water released. Aerial drone inspection identified approximate spill boundary.
High-volume produced water pump overpressure event. Relief valve discharge directed to soil. 900 BBL released.
Third-party pipeline rupture from construction equipment contact. 1,300 BBL produced water released across caliche road and adjacent soil.
Wellhead Christmas tree seal failure. 265 BBL produced water accumulated in secondary containment and overflowed.
